untranslatable_reason: This signal requires event-driven triggering (acquisition proposal announcement, approval, or cancellation) and multi-leg position management (simultaneous long target + short acquirer with dynamic hedge ratios). These are fundamentals/events not available in the allowed technical indicators list. The signal also requires tracking of two separate securities with a calculated exchange ratio, which exceeds the scope of single-instrument rule conditions. A proper implementation requires an event data source and portfolio-level position management outside the backtest engine's indicator-based rule framework.
translation_notes: MCMILLAN_DRY_196 is a merger arbitrage strategy requiring external event data (M&A announcements, shareholder votes, deal approvals/rejections) and multi-asset coordination. The allowed indicators are purely technical/price-based and cannot capture the event-driven nature of this trade. Recommend implementing this strategy with: (1) an event ingestion layer for M&A catalysts, (2) portfolio-level rules for ratio spreads, and (3) deal-tracking data feeds rather than candlestick technicals.
